rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND CRONIN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 16, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Ford Motor Company¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Cronin was exposed to asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant. The May 10, 2011 deposition testimony of Mr. Aahl precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES FEDUSKA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel -Civil
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 16, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Quintrall & Associates¿ motion to be relieved as counsel for Helmer Truck Parts, Inc. is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion For Costs And Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 16, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿s motion for costs and attorney fees is denied. Defendant¿s notice of motion states that the basis for the requested award of costs is CCP 1032(b). An award of costs under this statute is determined not on a motion by the party seeking costs, but on a motion to tax costs filed by the party opposing an award of costs. Plaintiffs filed a motion to tax costs and thus the court will decide defendant¿s entitlement to costs under 1032(b) when ruling on the motion to tax. Defendant¿s request for fees is based on CCP 2033.420. That section only permits an award of fees against a party, not the party¿s counsel, and is based on the predicate that the party unreasonably denied a request for admission. (Estate of Manuel (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 400). No evidence has been submitted that any of the plaintiffs were aware of, much less participated in, the denials of RFAs 1-3, even assuming that those denials were unreasonable. There is no evidence that plaintiffs¿ responses to either the RFAs or similarly-worded special interrogatories were verified. 2033.420, by its language is intended to impose costs of proof on a party for the party¿s conduct, not the conduct of the party¿s counsel. A separate and independent basis for denial of the requested fees is the failure of defendant in its moving papers to provide any evidentiary support about the nature of the services for which the fees were incurred other than an insufficient conclusory sentence. While evidentiary support for the fees was provided with the reply papers, plaintiffs were denied the opportunity to provide written opposition to that evidentiary support. = (220/HEK)
